Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Pyraloidea; Crambidae; Musotima
Assembly: The assembly ilMusNiti1.1 is based on 45x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Aequornithes; Sphenisciformes; Spheniscidae; Aptenodytes
Assembly: The King penguin is an iconic species of the Southern Ocean and is currently being developed as a model-in-the-wild for understanding evolution. We present a chromosome-level assembly of the King penguin (bAptPat1) which is based on 58.5 Gb of Pacbio Revio data and 126 Gbp of Arima Hi-C data. The initial PacBio assembly generation was performed with Hifiasm and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The Nextflow pipeline curationpretext was used to create Hi-C contact maps for manual curation. Manual curation was conducted using PretextView and potential microchromosome scaffolds were identified using MicroFinder. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
